what: Read Claude Code config file (~/.claude.json) and extract project paths
why: Claude Code stores all projects a user has worked with — this is our discovery source

success_handling.criteria: Config file read and parsed, projects array extracted (may be empty)

decisions.rationale: Avoids reading real ~/.claude.json in test environment; allows fixture-based testing

decisions.reason: Always returns success with empty array on errors
decisions.rationale: Discovery is best-effort — failure to read config should not block project listing

decisions.reason: Normalize and deduplicate paths before returning
decisions.rationale: Claude Code stores paths with inconsistent separators (C:\... vs C:/...) on Windows, causing the same project to appear multiple times. path.normalize() canonicalises separators; a Set tracks seen paths to drop duplicates.
